title: In transit
summary: Too small in the seat, too aware of everything forgotten, then Hong Kong green under fog and the strange feeling of moving through a sky full of other lives.

blocks.caption: The feeling of being too small in your seat, squashed by the person next to you.

blocks.text: Moving around constantly, thinking: did you pack everything? And then, yes, realising you definitely did not pack your tent poles.

blocks.story: Flying into Hong Kong, everything was green but covered in fog. It felt like a strange metaphor for the trip itself — always thinking about what you will see next, but never quite seeing all of it at once. Then the transfer: more people passing you, more bags, more gates, more waiting. Weird to think about all the people in the sky at one time. Something you never really consider when you are lying on the grass on a summer day, staring upward, or wondering if someone thousands of kilometres away might be doing the same thing. Some strange sort of universal blanket.

details.label: Ate
details.value: Delicious aeroplane corn fritters and too many KitKats.

details.label: Watched
details.value: The Housemaid, Merrily We Go Along, and Anastasia.

details.label: Map pins
details.value: Sydney. Hong Kong. The walking city at the end of the day.

blocks.caption: Flying into Hong Kong surrounded by green, but fog — seeing enough to know there was more behind it.

blocks.text: Finish: the walking city. Surprise — beautiful and quiet, people finishing for the day. Much sleep was needed.
